notes: Frozen bundle for the top-5 truths eval. Built from the Browserbase production/sidebar fixture family in experiments/activegraph-coldstart. It intentionally preserves conflicting funding evidence.

sources.title: Browserbase enrichment profile
sources.text: Browserbase is the complete platform to build and deploy agents that browse and interact with the web like humans. The enrichment profile says Browserbase has $128.5M in total funding, 4 prior funding rounds, about 50 employees, and 110% year-over-year employee growth.

sources.title: Built In Series B report
sources.text: Built In reports Browserbase raised a $40M Series B led by Notable Capital, with CRV and Kleiner Perkins participating. It says Browserbase serves over 1,000 companies and more than 20,000 developers, and launched Director, a no-code automation tool.

sources.title: SiliconANGLE Series B report
sources.text: SiliconANGLE reports Notable Capital led Browserbase's $40M Series B. It says the company was valued at $300M, four times its September valuation. It describes a platform that spins up thousands of browsers in a fraction of a second, compatible with Puppeteer and Selenium, and says Stagehand lets developers combine scripts with AI agents.

sources.title: Upstarts Media Series B and traction report
sources.text: Upstarts Media reports Browserbase has 1,000+ customers including Commure, 11x, Perplexity, and Vercel; 20,000+ developers; 50M browser sessions in 2025; and more than $3M in revenue as of the Series B, mostly from team expansion. It says Browserbase uses consumption-based pricing and hired its first salesperson on the day of the Series B announcement.

sources.title: Pulse 2 Series A report
sources.text: Pulse 2 reports Browserbase raised a $21M Series A co-led by CRV and Kleiner Perkins, with Okta Ventures and Reed McGinley-Stempel. It says this came less than nine months after a $6.5M seed. It also says Browserbase launched Stagehand, an open-source AI-powered web automation SDK, at the Series A.

sources.title: AgentMarketCap technical analysis
sources.text: AgentMarketCap describes Browserbase as infrastructure for headless browser cloud and web-browsing agents. It says Stagehand exposes act, extract, and observe natural language commands above Playwright. It lists competitors including Steel.dev, Playwright-MCP, and Browserless. It also claims Browserbase raised a $27M Series A, conflicting with the $21M Series A report.

sources.title: Notable Capital investment note
sources.text: Notable Capital says it led Browserbase's Series B. Glenn Solomon says a great service that anticipates developer needs can be a compelling value proposition. Founder Paul Klein IV analogizes Browserbase to infrastructure companies like Vercel, Stripe, and Twilio.

sources.title: VC News Daily seed report
sources.text: VC News Daily reports Browserbase raised a $6.5M seed led by Kleiner Perkins, with Basecase Capital and AI Grant. It says the seed was announced on 2024-06-07.

sources.title: Lowenstein Series B confirmation
sources.text: Lowenstein Sandler confirms it represented Notable Capital as lead investor in Browserbase's Series B financing round.

sources.title: Browserbase product surface from production card
sources.text: The production card describes Browserbase as managed headless browser cloud for AI agents and developers. Customers connect via Playwright, Puppeteer, or Selenium to a serverless cloud that provisions isolated Chromium sessions with CAPTCHA solving, stealth fingerprinting, proxy rotation, compliance controls, and global data-center distribution. Stagehand adds natural-language act, extract, and observe commands above Playwright, while Director generates automation scripts from natural-language prompts for non-developers.
